text: The narrative of decentralization in crypto is critically examined, with Hyperliquid cited as an example of a project that prioritizes efficiency over decentralization, operating with a small validator set and centralized decision-making for practical reasons. This reflects a broader industry trend where decentralization is sometimes a strategic narrative rather than a strict principle, especially in infrastructure-layer products.
author: elfa_2043731304089563284

text: The crypto community is debating Hyperliquid's valuation and future price trajectory, with some analysts arguing that despite its impressive fundamentals and growth, HYPE is not undervalued due to large unvested token supply, regulatory risks, keyman risk, and the challenge of finding marginal buyers at current prices. Others highlight the project's unique position as a 24/7 derivatives exchange and its deflationary tokenomics as bullish factors.
author: elfa_2043801444764074029

text: Hyperliquid is expanding its ecosystem with new features such as the first Nigerian Naira ($NGN) offramp allowing fast fiat withdrawals, integration with DeFi bridges showing strong net inflows, and the launch of new perpetual markets like Paragon indexes (BTC.D, TOTAL2, OTHERS) powered by Hyperliquid. These developments enhance accessibility and trading options for users globally.
author: elfa_2043687526083264882

text: Jeff Yan, founder of Hyperliquid, has a compelling personal story of self-taught physics, winning a gold medal at the International Physics Olympiad, and building one of the largest anonymous crypto trading firms before founding Hyperliquid. He chose to reject venture capital funding to maintain the project's integrity and has built a highly profitable, lean team that is reshaping finance with a vision to 'house all of finance'.
author: elfa_2043733915375153364

text: Hyperliquid has introduced priority fees on its mainnet, allowing traders to pay in HYPE tokens for order and gossip priority, with a portion of fees burned, creating constant demand and deflationary pressure on the token. This mechanism incentivizes market makers and traders to use HYPE, potentially increasing token value as trading volume grows.
author: elfa_2043631928071393645

text: The HIP-3 protocol on HyperliquidX shows significant open interest growth with over $2 billion in open interest, led by on-chain perpetuals for oil and the S&P 500. However, volume and revenue growth have not matched open interest increases, with some volume growth coming at the expense of crypto perpetuals. The community remains bullish but cautious, noting that revenue is generated from fees on volume, not open interest, and that some volume may be sustained by subsidies.
author: elfa_2043818059492929778

text: Hyperliquid, a crypto trading exchange and blockchain platform, has reached $5 billion in TVL and is processing over $4 trillion in volume with markets for oil, silver, and the S&P 500 trading 24/7. The project is notable for its lean team of 11, no venture capital funding, and $900 million profit generated last year. Founder Jeff Yan declined a $100 million funding round to maintain protocol purity. Hyperliquid is gaining significant traction with growing open interest, priority fee mechanisms that burn tokens, and expanding institutional and retail adoption, although some caution exists regarding valuation and future marginal buyers.
author: elfa_2043796370444673095

text: Yesterday's ideas have been invalidated. ⁃ Saylors Friday raise spiking BTC back to up 72k ⁃ Strc's Monday raise is nuts and over half a bil, still have Tuesdays to go and likely just as big ⁃ Trump's toning softening and trying to push the agenda of more ships going through SOH ⁃ Iran looking into abandoning uranium enrichment (nuclear) but this seems highly unlikely, probably false headlines ⁃ SPX ripping and U.S is putting on a front they're selling domestic oil now Ended up roundtripping a good amount of my BTC short and Oil long and got stopped out while asleep. Expensive sleep and sucks as I had my invalidations but wasn't able to act timely enough on them. That's perps unfortunately and just have to keep shooting. Current position: Long BTC @ 72.8k I woke up and longed as soon as I could. This time last month we saw how impactful Strc's final days of bidding were to 76k. I tried to front run the final day by shorting last month, except bidding 400-500 mil spot in a single day steam rolled me so I'm aware of how much momentum these final two days will have. Best not to get in the way and enjoy the free ride. Since there's so much volume coming in from Strc you know the drawdowns will be fairly limited and you need to enter higher than you'd like as you'll get left behind. Keep in mind I typically trade on 50x lev and requires a strong pulse on the 5 min charts. I wouldn't recommend this if you're only online for half of the day and not able to monitor news and flows constantly. This is more so for my mental clarity describing how I'm executing the position. With how hard price is going to swing on LTF's you're incredibly susceptible to titling which is more damaging than the profits/losses themselves. Timeline wise I'm looking to be long BTC going into Wednesday and how long I hold that position during Wednesday depends on the Strc raise. If it's similar numbers to Monday then you don't want to fight flows. Target wise if Tuesdays raise is similar numbers (500 mil ball park) then I'm looking for 76-77k on Wednesday granted conditions don't change. Thursday will be a good inflection point as second round of negotiations will take place in Islamabad. Invalidations: ⁃ U.S blockade in the SOH being met with physical escalation (ceasefire agreement collapsing) ⁃ Thursdays talks delayed due to some type of physical attack or ships being blown up For now it seems like Trump has soft taco'd and will likely hold this stance until Thursdays meeting. Even if his sentiment changes I don't think it affects BTC much. Strc's bid is insane and will absorb any negative news other than the collapse of the ceasefire over the next two days imo.

text: Detangling Tokenization of RWAs How Real World Asset (RWA) tokenization has exploded from $5B in 2023 to over $25B in 2026, with three distinct models emerging: > Digitally Native Tokenized Products: These tokens provide direct exposure to the underlying assets, it entitles the holder to the full ownership rights and protections. > Synthetic Exposure Tokens: Rather than the token providing direct exposure to the underlying asset, it operates like a swap, providing pass-through of the economic outcomes generated by the asset. > Digital Twin Tokens: Ownership of the asset is awarded and recorded in traditional, off-chain form and the token functions as a "receipt" showing that the token holder owns the off-chain asset. Source:

text: SEC Clarifies Certain DeFi UIs Can Operate Without Broker-Dealer Registration The SEC's Division of Trading and Markets said certain crypto trading interfaces, including DeFi front-ends, wallet extensions, and mobile apps, may operate without broker-dealer registration if specific conditions are met. These include no order routing, no investment advice, no custody of user assets, and only fixed, neutral fee structures. The guidance applies to self-custodial wallet interfaces and will remain effective for five years unless superseded by further SEC action. Galaxy's Alex Thorn says today's SEC staff guidance shows the agency can move crypto market structure forward without waiting for Congress. He added the move may pave the way for future innovation exemptions covering tokenized securities trading via AMMs and other decentralized apps, while noting CLARITY still needs to be codified into law. — link

text: RaveDAO's price has surged 3765% in the last 7 days, with major trading volume concentrated in Binance and OKX Futures, exceeding $13 billion. Analysts have described this as a potential "deception tactic" or classic pump-and-dump setup, where the deposits created apparent selling pressure to lure short sellers, followed by aggressive spot buying that triggered a violent short squeeze in the token's extremely low circulating supply (~23–25%). According to an analysis by the Evening Trader Group, RAVE's surge from $0.30 to $10 in just three days was a classic market manipulation operation. However, the real profits were not generated through simply pumping the price, but rather by liquidating the massive short positions that had been lured into the market. The entity behind the operation first transferred 30.58 million USD worth of RAVE to Bitget, creating the illusion that a major sell-off was imminent. This successfully induced a large number of traders to open short positions. Over the following two days, approximately 32 million USD worth of RAVE was withdrawn back to the blockchain, while the spot price was aggressively pumped, resulting in the complete liquidation of all trapped short positions. — link

text: QCP Market Colour, 13 April 2026 Fade the Blockade? Markets were hoping for a deal, but positioned for disappointment. US–Iran talks broke down over the weekend, pushing oil back above $100 and triggering a risk-off move. BTC ran into resistance at $74k, while ETH slipped from $2,330 to $2,180 as near-term expectations reset. Trump has escalated with blockade threats on Hormuz, while Iran signalled potential disruption via Bab el-Mandeb. The risk is clear, but enforcement is not. Any attempt to intercept major shipping flows, particularly involving China, would risk a broader escalation that markets are not priced for. That scepticism is showing up in crypto. Implied vols and skew have normalised toward pre-conflict levels, and both BTC and ETH continue to absorb geopolitical noise, pointing to a steady underlying bid. ETF flows remain supportive, with IBIT seeing $612.1m in inflows over the past week. With enforcement flagged from 10:00 ET, the focus now shifts from rhetoric to execution. After multiple delays, credibility is becoming part of the trade. Read more:

text: So inspired by the BPN Go One More Backyard Ultramarathon Runners run a fixed loop of 6.7km every hour, on the hour. Finish the loop as fast or as slow as you want (must be <60 mins), then use remaining time in the hour to rest + eat Race continues indefinitely until only 1 runner is left. These guys have been running 62 laps (62 hours) over 405km with barely any sleep Amazing what the human body can do when you push it to the limit

text: Just In: Hackers minted 1 billion DOT tokens on the Ethereum mainnet and then sold them off. According to Certik, the attack was primarily due to a Hyperbridge gateway vulnerability, which allowed attackers to forge messages and manipulate the administrator of a Polkadot token contract on Ethereum, profiting approximately $237k. Due to the low liquidity, the losses were relatively small. South Korean exchanges Upbit and Bithumb have suspended DOT deposits and withdrawals. Polkadot said an issue has affected Hyperbridge's Ethereum gateway contract, impacting only DOT bridged to Ethereum via Hyperbridge, while native DOT, Polkadot, and its parachains remain unaffected; Hyperbridge has been paused pending investigation. — link
